title: Imprint office-memory models (Duties/Sources/Decisions/Meetings/Notes) + cited vector retrieval
context: The Imprint layer is the office memory: it stores the working memory of the office -- duties, sources, decisions, meetings, and notes -- so faculty answers are grounded in cited, factual context rather than blank-slate LLM output. Entities: Duty, Source (citation + provenance), Decision/Vote, Meeting/Event, and Note -- all tenant-scoped, sensitive fields encrypted. Retrieval via pgvector cosine similarity with keyword fallback assembles a cited context block that every faculty answer must carry. This is the foundation of the grounded-vs-blank-slate thesis.
objective: Implement the Imprint office-memory data layer with Duty, Source, Decision/Vote, Meeting/Event, and Note models (1536-dim embeddings via pgvector), cosine retrieval with keyword fallback, and a cited-context-block assembler -- tested on fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Duty, Source (citation + provenance), Decision/Vote, Meeting/Event, and Note models implemented with 1536-dim vector embeddings
pgvector cosine retrieval with keyword fallback returns relevant records for fixture queries
Retrieval assembles a cited context block (each item carries its Source citation); tested on a fixture set
All rows tenant-scoped; sensitive fields encrypted per task 005 baseline
Unit tests cover retrieval accuracy on fixtures
output: A merged PR implementing the Imprint office-memory layer with all five models, pgvector cosine retrieval, keyword fallback, cited-context-block assembly, and fixture tests.
